content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: Please own shell_completion.py and, alongside your with-statement wrapping fix, ensure every Context created during shell completion has validate_nesting=False explicitly passed. Specifically: in _resolve_context() (~line 532-586), every call to a MultiCommand/Command's make_context(...) (or Context(...) construction) used while walking the resolved command path must pass validate_nesting=False as a kwarg. Also check resolve_ctx / any other helper in shell_completion.py that constructs a Context (e.g. functions that build contexts for completion items) — they must also pass validate_nesting=False. This prevents spurious RuntimeError from my new nested-context validation feature (Context.__init__ gets a new validate_nesting: bool = False param in core.py; when True, close()/__exit__ raises RuntimeError if child sub-contexts made via _make_sub_context() haven't been closed yet). Since shell completion walks/holds multiple nested contexts without necessarily closing them in strict order, forcing validate_nesting=False there avoids false positives. If you use 'with Context(...)' the __exit__ will call close() — just make sure validate_nesting=False is set on those Context instances so close() doesn't validate nesting.
note: make_context / Context(...) calls in _resolve_context and any other context-constructing helper in this file

type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: Implemented: Context.__init__ validate_nesting param (default False), self.validate_nesting attribute, self._children tracking list, _make_sub_context() now passes validate_nesting=self.validate_nesting to children and tracks them in self._children when enabled, close() raises RuntimeError listing open children when validate_nesting=True and children remain, and removes itself from parent._children on close. Also applied your docstring tweak in Context.exit(). All existing tests pass (558 passed, pre-existing unrelated failure in test_basic.py due to pytest version). Ready for your shell_completion.py changes — let me know when you're done so I can do a final combined check.

content:
type: SPEC
files: src/click/core.py
spec: We're in separate isolated working directories/worktrees (each agent gets its own /workspace/repo sandbox) — my core.py edits exist only in my sandbox and won't appear in yours. This is expected per the submission protocol: each of us writes our own patch.txt independently and the harness evaluates/merges separately. You don't need core.py's validate_nesting to actually exist in your sandbox to finish your feature — just write your shell_completion.py changes assuming the Context.__init__ signature I specified (validate_nesting: bool = False, inherited by _make_sub_context, enforced in close()/__exit__). If you need to locally verify your shell_completion.py changes pass validate_nesting=False without errors, you can temporarily stub validate_nesting as an accepted no-op kwarg on Context in YOUR sandbox for testing purposes only, then revert that stub before generating your final patch.txt (since core.py is not yours to submit). My core.py diff is complete and tested on my end (558 passed). Let me know if you need the exact diff text pasted here for reference.
note: clarifying sandbox isolation, not a blocker for your implementation
